title: Beaver Creek bridge — linked HEC-RAS unsteady (plan 03 / u02 hydrograph)

parity_program.notes: Restart 2026: layered diagnostic (mapping → steady → unsteady). Observed HWM is dev proxy only. HDF WSEL(t) required for certification.

scope.notes: Classic HEC-RAS Beaver Creek / Kentwood reach with inline bridge at RM 5.4 (9 piers, WSPRO). ~57 cross sections.

linked_project.notes: Plan 03: Unsteady 100-yr event. Computation Interval 2MIN; upstream Q input 1HOUR in u02; UNET theta=1.0; DS boundary friction slope 0.002.

stream1d.notes: Piecewise deck + 9 piers + WSPRO (method 4) from g01; explicit BU/BD face cuts and per-face ineffective from XS descriptions; three-segment friction (weighting=1); Q(t) resampled hourly→2MIN; downstream_bc_type=2 friction-slope normal depth on DS XS; theta=1.0 from plan; initial_wsel from solve_steady at initial Q. High-flow bridge uses explicit post-step fallback (B3).

reference.notes: Observed HWM = peak WSEL at each RM from RAS plan 03 (dev convenience). Certification requires HDF extract at ≥10 checkpoint RMs — see extract_hdf_wsel.py.

compare.notes: Compare terminal WSEL (end of 48 h recession) — reference JSON is terminal from plan 03 HDF, not peak. Use max_wsel only after extracting peak WSEL(t) from HDF.
